techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: Exploit command injection vulnerability in Flask web application running inside a Docker container on port 8080. HTTP POST to /cmd endpoint with arbitrary shell commands.
expected_alert_findings: WAF alert on command injection pattern in POST body
VPC Flow Logs: unexpected inbound traffic to port 8080

attack_surface: EC2 instance running vulnerable Flask app on port 8080 (0.0.0.0/0)

techniques.opsec_notes: Container exploitation generates ZERO CloudTrail events. Only VPC Flow Logs or container-level logging would capture this.

techniques.name: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.description: Steal EC2 instance role temporary credentials from IMDSv1 endpoint (169.254.169.254) from inside a Docker container. IMDSv1 enabled with hop_limit=2 allows Docker bridge access.
expected_alert_findings: GuardDuty: UnauthorizedAccess:IAMUser/InstanceCredentialExfiltration.OutsideAWS (when credentials used from external IP)

attack_surface: EC2 instance with IMDSv1 enabled (HttpTokens=optional, HopLimit=2)

techniques.opsec_notes: IMDSv1 credential theft is completely invisible to CloudTrail. The stolen credentials appear in CloudTrail only when USED from an external IP.

techniques.name: Resource Hijacking
techniques.description: Deploy XMRig cryptominer configuration and simulation script inside the compromised container. Simulates the resource hijacking aspect of the real SCARLETEEL campaign.
data_plane_actions: HTTP POST /cmd to write /tmp/miner.sh and execute it
expected_alert_findings: GuardDuty: CryptoCurrency:EC2/BitcoinTool.B!DNS (if real mining DNS queries made)
techniques.opsec_notes: SIMULATED ONLY — no actual mining occurs. XMRig config files written to /tmp/ on container.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
techniques.description: Use stolen EC2 instance role temporary credentials to perform AWS API calls from an external IP. Proves the credentials were exfiltrated from IMDS.

target: IAM resources, S3 buckets, Lambda functions, CloudTrail trail

cleanup_actions: Revoke role sessions via DateLessThan condition in inline deny policy
techniques.opsec_notes: sourceIPAddress in CloudTrail will show external attacker IP, not the EC2 instance IP — the primary detection vector.

techniques.name: Cloud Service Discovery
techniques.description: Enumerate S3 buckets (ListBuckets, ListObjectsV2), Lambda functions (ListFunctions, GetFunction, ListVersionsByFunction, GetPolicy, ListAliases, ListTags, ListEventSourceMappings), and CloudTrail trails (DescribeTrails) using stolen instance role credentials.
expected_alert_findings: SIEM: 5+ Lambda read APIs within 30 seconds from same principal
techniques.opsec_notes: Lambda enumeration burst (7 API calls in <5 seconds) is a detectable automation fingerprint.

techniques.name: Data from Cloud Storage
techniques.description: Exfiltrate objects from S3 buckets including terraform.tfstate containing IAM access keys. Also exfiltrate Lambda source code via presigned URL returned by GetFunction.

expected_audit_events.note: Data event — only logged if S3 data event logging enabled

expected_alert_findings: CRITICAL GAP: GetObject is a data event — invisible without explicit S3 data event logging
techniques.opsec_notes: GetObject calls are INVISIBLE in CloudTrail management events. Attacker can exfiltrate entire buckets without a trace unless S3 data events are enabled.

techniques.name: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
techniques.description: Disable CloudTrail logging via cloudtrail:StopLogging to prevent further audit trail creation. The attacker identifies the trail name via DescribeTrails first.
expected_alert_findings: CloudWatch alarm on StopLogging metric filter
techniques.opsec_notes: StopLogging is the most detectable single event in the entire attack chain. It should NEVER appear in normal operations.

techniques.name: Credentials In Files
techniques.description: Extract IAM long-term access key credentials from terraform.tfstate file stored in S3 bucket. The state file contains plaintext aws_iam_access_key resources.

expected_audit_events.note: Data event — only logged if S3 data event logging enabled

expected_alert_findings: CRITICAL GAP: terraform.tfstate GetObject is a data event — invisible in management events

bait: terraform.tfstate containing IAM access key for scarleteel-secondary-user

techniques.opsec_notes: Real-world SCARLETEEL exfiltrated terraform.tfstate files from S3 state buckets across multiple accounts. This is a high-fidelity simulation.
